name: D+Q 20-week diarrhea adverse-event result
arm_details: Extended Data Table 1 reports the diarrhea row with a blank control cell and 7 (23%) in the D+Q cell. The blank control cell is preserved as not reported or below-table-threshold ambiguity, not encoded as zero.

effect.raw_text: Diarrhea row in Extended Data Table 1: control cell blank; D+Q 7 (23%).

analysis.effect_note: No comparative diarrhea effect was calculated because the control cell is blank in a table restricted to events reported in more than one participant for each group.

event_specific_counts.interpretation: The source table leaves the control diarrhea cell blank under a threshold for adverse events reported in more than one participant; this is not encoded as zero.
The source table reports diarrhea in 7 D+Q participants, 23% of the arm.

zero_handling.rationale: The retained table does not state whether the blank control cell means zero events or a count below the table's reporting threshold.

provenance.note: Extended Data Table 1 diarrhea row reports a blank control cell and D+Q 7 (23%); the footnote says results are adverse events reported in more than one participant for each group.
Safety paragraph identifies gastrointestinal complaints among prominent adverse events and states that no serious or severe adverse events related to D+Q administration occurred.

statement: In the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT publication table, diarrhea was reported for 7 of 30 D+Q participants (23%); the matching control cell was blank and is not treated as a zero count.
caveats: The publication table does not provide an explicit control diarrhea count; the blank control cell is preserved as zero/not-reported ambiguity.
No risk ratio, risk difference, confidence interval, standard error, or variance is available from this row without unsupported zero handling.
The open-label design may affect subjective adverse-event reporting.
